evals.prompt: I've never used Kamal before. Walk me through installing it and getting my Rails app deployed to a fresh server for the first time.
evals.expected_output: Should give an ordered walk-through: install with `gem install kamal` (or note the dockerized alternative if no Ruby), run `kamal init` to create config/deploy.yml, .kamal/secrets, and sample hooks in .kamal/hooks, edit config/deploy.yml (service, image, servers, registry, builder, env), fill in .kamal/secrets, then run `kamal setup` for the first deploy. Should explain that setup installs Docker if missing, boots accessories, and deploys. Should cross-reference the configuration, secrets, deploying, or registry skills.
assertions: Recommends installing with `gem install kamal`
Has the user run `kamal init`
States that `kamal init` creates config/deploy.yml, .kamal/secrets, and sample hooks in .kamal/hooks
Walks through editing config/deploy.yml with service, image, servers, registry, and env keys
Has the user run `kamal setup` for the first deploy
Explains setup installs Docker, boots accessories, and deploys the app
Cross-references at least one related skill (configuration, secrets, deploying, or registry)

evals.prompt: how do i install kamal if i don't have ruby on my machine?
evals.expected_output: Should explain you can run Kamal from a Docker container using a shell alias (the `docker run ... ghcr.io/basecamp/kamal:latest` alias), with the correct macOS or Linux variant. Should note the limitations of the dockerized path: the secret adapters are unavailable, host environment variables are not passed in unless injected, and it forwards the SSH agent only. May link to the dockerized-install reference.
assertions: Explains Kamal can be run via a Docker container alias
Shows the `docker run` alias using ghcr.io/basecamp/kamal:latest
Mentions at least one limitation (secret adapters unavailable, host env vars not passed, or SSH agent forwarding only)
Does not claim the dockerized path is fully equivalent to the Ruby install

evals.expected_output: Should state that `kamal init` creates the files needed to deploy: config/deploy.yml (the main configuration), the .kamal/secrets file, and sample hooks in .kamal/hooks. Should not invent additional files.
assertions: Lists config/deploy.yml
Lists .kamal/secrets
Lists sample hooks in .kamal/hooks
Does not invent files or behaviors beyond these three

evals.prompt: I just ran kamal init. What do I actually put in config/deploy.yml to get started?
evals.expected_output: Should suggest reading the existing config/deploy.yml first, then walk through a minimal config with service, image, servers, registry (username and password), builder, and env. Should explain that servers is a list (implicitly the web role), that registry/password references a secret by name rather than a literal value, and that env/secret names come from the secrets file. Should reference the configuration skill for the full key list.
assertions: Suggests reading the current config/deploy.yml before editing
Covers service, image, servers, registry, and env keys
Notes that registry/password references a secret name, not a literal password
Notes a plain servers list is assigned to the web role
Cross-references the configuration skill for the full set of keys

evals.prompt: My servers are brand new and don't have Docker installed. Will Kamal set that up, or do I have to do it manually?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that `kamal setup` installs Docker on all servers if it has permission and Docker is not already installed (using get.docker.com, which needs root SSH access). Should also mention `kamal server bootstrap` as the explicit way to set up Docker, which checks whether Docker is installed and attempts to install it via get.docker.com if not.
assertions: States that `kamal setup` installs Docker if it has permission and Docker is not already installed
Mentions get.docker.com as the install mechanism
Mentions `kamal server bootstrap` as the explicit way to set up Docker
Does not invent a separate Docker-install command

evals.prompt: what's the difference between kamal setup and kamal deploy? when do i use each?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that `kamal setup` is for a fresh host: it installs Docker if needed, boots all accessories, and then deploys the app. `kamal deploy` is for subsequent deploys (or when the servers already have Docker installed) and just builds and deploys the current checked-out version. Should advise running setup once first, then deploy thereafter.
assertions: Describes setup as the first-time / fresh-host command
Notes setup installs Docker, boots accessories, and deploys
Describes deploy as the command for subsequent releases or servers that already have Docker
Advises running setup first, then deploy for later releases

evals.prompt: How do I wire up my Docker registry password as a secret before the first deploy?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that registry/password in config/deploy.yml references a secret name (e.g. KAMAL_REGISTRY_PASSWORD). The user sets that value in their environment and edits .kamal/secrets to read it, e.g. `KAMAL_REGISTRY_PASSWORD=$KAMAL_REGISTRY_PASSWORD`. Should note .kamal/secrets lines map a name to an environment variable or command output. Should cross-reference the secrets skill for vault adapters (1Password, Bitwarden, AWS, etc.).
assertions: Explains registry/password references a secret name like KAMAL_REGISTRY_PASSWORD
Shows editing .kamal/secrets with `KAMAL_REGISTRY_PASSWORD=$KAMAL_REGISTRY_PASSWORD`
Notes secrets can come from an environment variable or command output
Cross-references the secrets skill for vault adapters
Does not reveal or hardcode a literal password
